{{CargoRelic
|name=Bronze Bull Head
|class_id=20446
|rank=Green
|affct=FAME
|source=
|description=This bronze bull head from the times of Shang and Zhou dynasties was unearthed at a heritage site in Jinsha, Chengdu. Its forehead is marked with a diamond pattern, the horns are covered in spirals, and the hollow neck can be hooked up to the bronze vessel. It is speculated to be a standing decoration on a large bronze vessel during the Shang and Zhou dynasties, and is of great significant value to the study of bronzeworking techniques in ancient Shu.
|fame=30
|art=16
|fth=17
|civ=14
|tech=13
|ss1=Per Organ implanted, Snail ATK +1<br>Organs Implanted: 40/40
|ss2=Per Organ implanted, Snail ATK +2<br>Organs Implanted: 40/40
|ss3=Per Organ implanted, Snail ATK +3<br>Organs Implanted: 40/40
|ss4=Per Organ implanted, Snail ATK +3<br>Organs Implanted: 40/40
|ss7=Per Organ implanted, Snail ATK +3<br>Organs Implanted: 40/40<br> (Awakened)
}}
